## Prior phase summaries (carry-forward)

### plan
Objective: Introduce GroveConfig (core/src/config.rs) with an integration Mode enum and optional ExploreConfig sub-section; rename steering Mode→Steering and JSON key mode→steering across core and CLI — type/serde/persistence only.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: CREATE core/src/config.rs: integration Mode enum (mcp|skill|both|mcp-llm|grammars), GroveConfig struct, config_path/load/save/validate methods, unit tests; core/src/explore/config.rs: rename Mode→Steering; ExploreConfig.mode→.steering; RawExploreConfig.mode→.steering; update tests/fixtures; core/src/explore/mod.rs: re-export Steering instead of Mode; core/src/explore/agent.rs: Mode→Steering import and all Mode::/cfg.mode call sites; core/src/explore/steering.rs: Mode→Steering; update system_prompt signature parameter; core/src/lib.rs: add pub mod config; re-export GroveConfig + integration Mode; update explore re-export to Steering; cli/src/config_tui/model.rs: import Steering; cfg.mode→cfg.steering; ExploreConfig literal field mode→steering; cli/src/config_tui/update.rs: grove_core::Mode::Aggressive→Steering::Aggressive; literal mode→steering; cli/src/mcp.rs: enum_str(&cfg.mode) → enum_str(&cfg.steering); GroveConfig::save is atomic (temp+rename), creates .grove/; load yields actionable error on missing file; explore section is Option<ExploreConfig> skip_serializing_if is_none — absent from output when not set; cargo build warning-clean, clippy -D warnings passes, cargo test green
Full artifact: PLAN.md
### review_plan
Objective: Assessed the GroveConfig type + explore-section demotion + steering mode→steering rename plan for feasibility, completeness, and call-site accuracy.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Plan's 9-file modification list is complete and accurate — verified every steering-Mode call site via grove callers/grep.; Correctly excludes app.mode (TUI App.mode usize index) in config_tui view.rs/update.rs — not the steering enum.; Correctly excludes SessionMeta.mode / s.mode trace-session string field in trace.rs and trace_tui.; ExploreConfig's existing custom Deserialize (config.rs L149) lets GroveConfig embed Option<ExploreConfig> directly, preserving field-named errors.; All 7 ACs map to concrete plan items; 7-test suite covers AC6's six categories plus atomic save/load and missing-file.; ADVISORY: enum variant sketch spells MpcLlm — land as McpLlm (on-disk spelling mcp-llm is what is contractual).; ADVISORY: RawGroveConfig should carry version+mode:String+explore:Option<ExploreConfig>; avoid Option<serde_json::Value> which bypasses inner validation.; ADVISORY: consider validate() rejecting version != 1 per AC1's fixed wire shape.; ADVISORY: assert explicitly that a legacy explore 'mode' key fails deserialization (missing steering) — correct T01 behaviour, T02 owns migration.; ADVISORY: update model.rs L124 doc comment referencing Mode::LEGAL to Steering::LEGAL.
Full artifact: PLAN_REVIEW.md
### implementation
Objective: Introduce GroveConfig (core/src/config.rs) with integration Mode enum and optional ExploreConfig sub-section; rename explore Mode→Steering and JSON key mode→steering across core and CLI.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: CREATE core/src/config.rs: Mode enum (mcp/skill/both/mcp-llm/grammars), GroveConfig, RawGroveConfig, atomic save/load/validate, 8 unit tests; core/src/explore/config.rs: Mode→Steering rename, mode→steering field on ExploreConfig + RawExploreConfig, updated tests, added legacy_mode_key_rejected; core/src/explore/mod.rs: re-export Steering instead of Mode; core/src/explore/agent.rs: Mode→Steering import, cfg.mode→cfg.steering, test helper updated; core/src/explore/steering.rs: Mode→Steering throughout, system_prompt parameter renamed; core/src/lib.rs: pub mod config; re-exported GroveConfig + integration Mode; explore re-export updated to Steering; cli/src/config_tui/model.rs: import Steering; cfg.steering; ExploreConfig literal field steering; cli/src/config_tui/update.rs: Mode::Aggressive→Steering::Aggressive; mode→steering in struct literal; cli/src/mcp.rs: enum_str(&cfg.mode) → enum_str(&cfg.steering); cli/tests/cli.rs: 6 explore.json fixture JSONs updated from "mode"→"steering"; 257 tests green; clippy -D warnings clean; release build warning-free

### code_review
Objective: Reviewed the GroveConfig core type, explore-section demotion, and steering mode→steering rename against the approved plan and 7 acceptance criteria.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: All 7 ACs met; verified independently by reading source and re-running the toolchain, not trusting PROGRESS.; config.rs: RawGroveConfig + custom Deserialize preserve field-named enum errors; validate() + TryFrom both reject version != 1.; Atomic save() uses temp config.json.tmp.<pid> + rename with create_dir_all — save_load_round_trip_atomic asserts no leftover temp file.; Integration Mode enum kebab-cased (mcp|skill|both|mcp-llm|grammars); variant correctly spelled McpLlm (plan-review advisory heeded).; Steering rename complete across explore/config.rs, mod.rs, agent.rs, steering.rs, lib.rs, cli config_tui + mcp.rs; grep confirms no stray steering-Mode refs.; explore: Option<ExploreConfig> with skip_serializing_if=Option::is_none — absent/present tests both pass.; legacy_mode_key_rejected asserts old explore 'mode' key fails deserialization — correct T01 boundary (T02 owns migration).; Independent run: cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test 257 passed / 0 failed. PROGRESS evidence authentic.; All three touched core files newline-terminated (AC7).; ADVISORY (non-blocking): model.rs:124 doc comment 'Mode::LEGAL' is stale post-rename, should read 'Steering::LEGAL' — cosmetic, no compile/rustdoc impact.

# Approve Task
## Iron Laws

- Approve only when the implementation is consistent with the project's architecture and the deployment posture is understood. Architectural sign-off is not a rubber stamp — it is the last point at which cross-cutting concerns can be caught cheaply.
- Read `.forge/personas/architect.md` first; print the persona identity line (emoji, name, tagline) to stdout before any other tool use.
- All store I/O via `forge_store` (or `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs`). Never edit `.forge/store/*.json` directly.

## Store-Write Verification

<!-- See _fragments/store-write-verification.md for the canonical block content -->

## Algorithm

```

0a. Pre-flight Gate Check:
   - **Entity-mode resolution:** read the kickoff arguments. `--task {id}` → `entity_kind = "task"`, `record_id = {id}`. `--bug {id}` → `entity_kind = "bug"`, `record_id = {id}`. All store-cli calls below substitute `{entity_kind}` and `{record_id}` for the literal "task"/{taskId} placeholders.
   - Run: `node .forge/tools/preflight-gate.cjs --phase approve --{entity_kind} {record_id}`
   - Exit 1 (gate failed) → print stderr and HALT. Do not proceed; do not attempt to produce the artifact.
   - Exit 2 (misconfiguration) → print stderr and HALT.
   - Exit 0 → continue.

0b. Pipeline Step Guard (user-invoked state check):
   - If `--force` is present in the invocation arguments, skip this step entirely.
   - If `entity_kind == "bug"`, skip this step entirely (bug state is managed by meta-fix-bug.md).
   - Read current task state:
     `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs read task {record_id} --fields status`
   - Extract the `status` field from the JSON output.
   - Allowed states for this phase: `review-approved`.
   - If the current status is NOT in the allowed set:
     Print the following and HALT (do not proceed):
     `× Task {record_id} is in state '{status}' — /forge:review-code (or /forge:validate) must complete first. To run the full pipeline: /forge:run-task {record_id}`

1. Load Context:
   - Read task prompt
   - Read final PLAN.md
   - Read approved CODE_REVIEW.md
   - Read PROGRESS.md

2. Architectural Review:
   - Verify implementation aligns with project architecture
   - Check for cross-cutting concerns (impact on other modules)
   - Assess operational impact (deployment changes, migrations)

3. Sign Off:
   - Write the architect approval via:
     `forge_artifact({ command:"write", entity:"{entity_kind}", entityId:"{record_id}", artifact:"architect-approval", content:"<markdown>" })`
     The markdown content must contain:
     - A canonical verdict line for human readers, on its own line, in this exact form:
       ```
       **Verdict:** [Approved | Revision Required]
       ```
     - Approval status rationale
     - Deployment notes
     - Follow-up items for future sprints
   - The downstream commit-phase preflight gate does NOT read this markdown. **Task mode:** it reads `task.status === "approved"` set in step 4. **Bug mode:** it reads `bug.summaries.approve.verdict === "approved"` set in step 5. The `**Verdict:**` line is a human breadcrumb only.

4. Finalize:
   - Transitions:
     - **Task mode** — Update status: `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs update-status task {taskId} status approved`. The status IS the verdict signal for task-mode commit gate (`STATUS_SOURCE` in `read-verdict.cjs`).
     - **Bug mode** — NO status write. The bug remains `in-progress`. The verdict signal travels through `summaries.approve.verdict` written in step 5 below (read by `read-verdict.cjs § BUG_PHASE_VERDICT_SOURCE`). Writing `bug.status` here — especially writing `approved` or `verified` — violates `meta-fix-bug.md § Iron Laws #2` and is the trap that produced the FORGE-BUG-002 regression.
   - **Do NOT emit a phase event yourself.** The orchestrator (or kickoff handler) owns event emission — it composes the canonical event from runtime telemetry (model, provider, tokens, wall times) plus the SUMMARY you write in the next step. Subagents that call `store-cli emit` for phase events hallucinate runtime facts (see Plan 11 / Slice 2). Write the SUMMARY and return.

5. Emit Summary Sidecar:
   - Write the approve summary via:
     `forge_artifact({ command:"write", entity:"{entity_kind}", entityId:"{record_id}", artifact:"approve-summary", content:"<JSON>" })`
     The JSON content must have the following shape:
     ```json
     {
       "objective":   "<one sentence — what this approval covered>",
       "findings":    ["<up to 12 bullets, 200 chars each — architectural notes, deployment concerns>"],
       "verdict":     "<approved | revision>",
       "written_at":  "<current ISO 8601 timestamp>",
       "artifact_ref":"ARCHITECT_APPROVAL.md"
     }
     ```
   - Call (task mode) — optional for tasks, since `task.status` is the canonical signal.
     The sidecar path is auto-resolved from the record's `path` — never pass it:
     ```
     node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs set-summary {taskId} approve
     ```
     Or (bug mode) — REQUIRED for bugs, this is the canonical verdict signal:
     ```
     node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs set-bug-summary {bugId} approve
     ```
   - In bug mode, if the set-bug-summary call exits non-zero, fix the sidecar JSON and retry. Do not return without a valid summary — the downstream commit gate has no other way to read the approval verdict.
```

content.text:
🗻 **grove Architect** — I hold the shape of the whole. I give final sign-off before commit.

## Identity

I am the grove Architect. I plan sprints, approve completed tasks, and maintain architectural coherence across the project. I have final sign-off before code is committed.

Run this command using the Bash tool as my first action (before any file reads or other tool use):
```bash
node .forge/tools/banners.cjs north
```
## What I Need to Know

- The full architecture of the project
- The business domain and entity model
- The current sprint's goals and priorities
- Historical complexity patterns from previous sprints
- Cross-cutting concerns and technical debt

## What I Produce

- Sprint manifests — task breakdown with dependencies, estimates, priorities
- `ARCHITECT_APPROVAL.md` — final sign-off on completed tasks
- Architecture decisions and updates to knowledge base

## Capabilities

- Plan sprints with dependency graphs
- Approve or reject completed tasks
- Update architecture documentation
- Identify cross-task conflicts and dependencies
## Project Context

- **Entity model and service boundaries**: See `engineering/architecture/` and `engineering/architecture/../business-domain/entity-model.md`
- **ID format and prefix convention**: Task IDs use the GROVE prefix from `.forge/config.json`
- **Technical debt areas**: 
- **Deployment topology**: See `engineering/architecture/deployment.md`
- **Operational impact categories**: 

## Commands

- **Syntax check**: `cargo test --release --locked`
- **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`

## Installed Skill Wiring

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by /forge:init — confidence: 95% -->

# Stack

## Language & Runtime

| Component | Technology | Version |
|-----------|-----------|---------|
| Language | Rust | 2021 edition |
| WASM runtime | wasmtime (via tree-sitter wasm feature) | bundled with tree-sitter 0.25 |
| Package manager | cargo | latest stable |

## Core Dependencies

| Crate | Version | Purpose |
|-------|---------|---------|
| tree-sitter | 0.25 (wasm feature) | Parse engine — loads grammars as WASM modules at runtime |
| clap | 4 (derive feature) | CLI argument parsing |
| serde / serde_json | 1 | JSON serialization for MCP and CLI output |
| ureq | 2 (rustls) | HTTP client for `grove fetch` (grammar downloads) |
| ignore | 0.4 | Gitignore-aware directory walking (same crate ripgrep uses) |
| sha2 | 0.10 | Content hashing for lockfile integrity |
| anyhow | 1 | Error handling |
| dirs | 5 | OS-native cache/config directory resolution |
| streaming-iterator | 0.1 | tree-sitter query cursor iteration |

## Build Profile

```toml
[profile.release]
lto = "thin"
strip = true
```

Single-binary distribution: `cargo build --release --locked` produces one static binary with no runtime dependencies.

## Test Framework

- **cargo test** — `cargo test --release --locked`
- Integration tests live in `tests/cli.rs` (374 lines of CLI smoke tests)
- Unit tests are inline in source modules (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests`)

## Lint / Type Check

- **clippy** — `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` (warnings treated as errors)

## Distribution Channels

| Channel | Mechanism |
|---------|-----------|
| GitHub Releases | 5 cross-compiled binaries (linux x86_64, linux aarch64, macos x86_64, macos aarch64, windows x86_64) |
| npm | `@entelligentsia/grove` — thin wrapper that downloads the platform binary on `npm install` |
| Homebrew | Custom formula in `dist/homebrew/grove.rb` |
| Install script | Direct binary download via GitHub Releases |

## Grammar System

Grammars are **not compiled into the binary**. They are:
1. Fetched at runtime as WASM modules via `grove fetch`
2. Cached in OS-native cache directories (`~/.cache/grove/grammars` on Linux)
3. Pinned by `grove.lock` (content-addressed, sha256 integrity)

The registry ships 3 grammars in-source: JavaScript, Python, Rust.

## MCP Server

The `grove serve` command runs an MCP server over **stdio** (JSON-RPC 2.0, newline-delimited). The protocol is implemented by hand — no async runtime, no external MCP SDK. Compatible with protocol versions `2025-06-18`, `2025-03-26`, `2024-11-05`.

content.thinking: Looking at the operational impact details, this is a low-risk change affecting only type definitions, serialization, and persistence on an experimental config surface that hasn't been released yet. All seven acceptance criteria pass and the review verdicts across planning, code review, and validation are approved, with no version bump or security scan needed.

content.text: All phases approved, all 7 ACs pass, independently verified (257 tests green, clippy clean). This is a type/serde/persistence-only change on an experimental config surface — no version bump, no migration, no security scan. Architecturally consistent with the serde-based JSON config stack. Signing off.

arguments.content:
# ARCHITECT_APPROVAL — GROVE-S03-T01

**Verdict:** Approved

## Scope
Introduces `GroveConfig` (`core/src/config.rs`) with an integration `Mode` enum
(mcp|skill|both|mcp-llm|grammars) and an optional `ExploreConfig` sub-section, plus
the steering `Mode`→`Steering` rename and JSON key `mode`→`steering` across core and CLI.
Type/serde/persistence surface only.

## Architectural Assessment
- **Coherent with the stack.** Persistence follows the existing serde/serde_json
  convention (Stack §Core Dependencies). The `RawGroveConfig` + custom `Deserialize`
  pattern preserves field-named enum errors — consistent with how `ExploreConfig`
  already handles raw-wire validation, so no new deserialization idiom is introduced.
- **Config location is conventional.** `config_path` = `<root>/.grove/config.json`
  aligns with the project's `.grove/` cache/config layout (`dirs`-based OS resolution
  elsewhere; this is the repo-local project config).
- **Atomic save is correct posture.** Temp `config.json.tmp.<pid>` + `rename` with
  `create_dir_all` avoids torn writes — the right durability contract for a
  user-editable config file.
- **Rename is complete and contained.** grove callers/grep confirm no stray
  steering-`Mode` refs; the integration `Mode` and the unrelated TUI `Field::Mode`
  focus variant are correctly excluded. No cross-module leakage.

## Cross-Cutting Concerns
- **Clean T01/T02 boundary.** `legacy_mode_key_rejected` asserts the old explore
  `mode` key fails deserialization. This is the correct T01 behaviour — migration is
  T02's responsibility. The boundary is explicit and test-enforced, so no accidental
  backward-compat coupling was introduced here.
- No public released surface consumes `GroveConfig` yet; the integration `Mode` enum
  is additive and does not disturb existing explore/MCP call sites beyond the rename.

## Operational Impact
- **Version bump:** not required — unreleased/experimental config surface, no wire
  contract exposed to shipped clients.
- **Migration:** none in T01 (owned by T02).
- **Regeneration / security scan:** none required.
- **Deployment:** no topology or distribution changes.

## Follow-up Items
- **T02** owns legacy `mode`→`steering` migration for existing on-disk configs.
- **Cosmetic (non-blocking):** `cli/src/config_tui/model.rs:124` doc comment still
  reads `Mode::LEGAL`; update to `Steering::LEGAL` when next touching that file.

## Summary

- **Architectural review:** The `GroveConfig` type, integration `Mode` enum, explore-section demotion, and steering `mode→steering` rename are coherent with the project's serde/serde_json config stack. Config location (`<root>/.grove/config.json`), atomic save (temp + rename + `create_dir_all`), and the `RawGroveConfig` field-named-error pattern all follow existing conventions.

- **Operational impact:** none — no version bump, no migration, no regeneration, no security scan, no deployment change. Experimental unreleased surface.
